description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a crab, featuring a broad, slightly domed carapace with a muted orange-brown coloration and subtle mottling. The crab has two prominent, asymmetrical claws (chelae), with one noticeably larger than the other, and four pairs of walking legs extending from the sides of its body. The underside is lighter in color, and the legs and claws display jointed segments with a rough, textured appearance. The carapace is smooth but shows some natural wear and patterning, typical of a crab's exoskeleton. No antennae or eyes are distinctly visible in these views, and the overall form is compact and robust, suitable for a marine crustacean. The object appears lifelike and would be recognizable as a food item in an urban seafood market or restaurant context.
description: A lifelike crab with a broad, orange-brown carapace, two large claws, and eight jointed legs, featuring a textured, natural exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad carapace, both claws, and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the larger claw and the segmentation of the walking legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full width of the carapace and the arrangement of the legs and claws from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the smaller claw and the curvature of the body and legs.
